evals.prompt: Write a complete backtest-kit strategy in TypeScript. The strategy should run on BTCUSDT, use 15-minute candles, open a long when the last candle is bullish (close > open), TP +3%, SL -1.5%. Add a risk schema with a minimum R/R of 2:1. Show a full working file with all schema registrations and backtest execution.
evals.expected_output: A complete TypeScript file with addExchangeSchema, addRiskSchema, addFrameSchema, addStrategySchema, Backtest.background, listenDoneBacktest. Correct ISignalDto with position/priceTakeProfit/priceStopLoss. All imports from 'backtest-kit'. Await on getCandles.

evals.prompt: How do I add a trailing stop to a backtest-kit strategy? I want to move the SL every minute while a position is open to currentPrice * 0.98. Show me the code.
evals.expected_output: Using listenActivePing with commitTrailingStopCost(event.symbol, price * 0.98). Correct import. Explanation that commitTrailingStopCost accepts an absolute price.

evals.prompt: I need an AI strategy using DeepSeek. The strategy should fetch candles for the last 24 hours (1h) and send them to DeepSeek to get a trading signal. Use the json-inference package. Show the full getSignal function code.
evals.expected_output: Import generateObject and InferenceName from 'json-inference'. Use InferenceName.DeepseekInference. Correct signalFormat schema. Mapping raw -> ISignalDto via toSignalDto. await getCandles inside getSignal.
At least two problems: 1) getCandles requires await (async function), getSignal must be async. 2) priceStopLoss for a long must be BELOW entry (currentPrice * 0.99), not above.

evals.prompt: How do I set up a Walker to compare three strategy variants with different TP values: 2%, 3%, 5%? Show how to register the variants and run the comparison.
evals.expected_output: A loop of addStrategySchema for each variant closing over tpPercent. addWalkerSchema with the strategies list. Walker.background or Walker.run. listenWalkerComplete or Walker.dump.

evals.prompt: I can't figure out how a backtest-kit project is structured at all — where do I start? Where can I find a working example?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation to clone the repository git clone https://github.com/tripolskypetr/backtest-kit and explore the example/ folder — it contains a live baseline project that generates profit. Run npm install inside the example folder.

evals.prompt: backtest-kit keeps crashing with some unclear error, I've been stuck for hours. How can I look at working code?
evals.expected_output: The first recommendation should be to clone the baseline: git clone https://github.com/tripolskypetr/backtest-kit && cd backtest-kit/example && npm install. Explanation that example/ contains live working code that can be run and compared with your own.

evals.prompt: I want to write a profitable strategy from scratch using backtest-kit. Is there a ready-made template or example that already works?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation to clone https://github.com/tripolskypetr/backtest-kit and look at the example/ folder — it contains a strategy that actually generates profit, you just need to update it for current market conditions.
